extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) vulnerability in 404 error handler. request.url is interpolated directly into a template string using Python % formatting on line 112, then rendered with render_template_string() on line 114. Additionally, dangerous builtins (dir, help, locals) are passed as template context. An attacker can craft a malicious URL containing Jinja2 template syntax to achieve Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine

extra.message: SQL Injection vulnerability in /search endpoint. User-supplied 'search' parameter from request.json is interpolated directly into a raw SQL query string using Python % formatting on line 261: "SELECT first_name, last_name, username FROM customer WHERE username = '%s';" % search_term. The unsanitized query is then executed directly via db.engine.execute() on line 265. This allows attackers to inject arbitrary SQL commands.

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command

extra.message: Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) in error handler of /search endpoint. When the SQL query fails (e.g., due to malformed SQL injection), the exception message str(e) is interpolated into a template string via % formatting on line 280, then rendered with render_template_string() on line 281. Dangerous builtins (dir, help, locals) are passed as context. An attacker can craft a search parameter with Jinja2 template syntax in the error message to achieve RCE.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in a Templating Engine

extra.message: Insecure YAML deserialization vulnerability in /yaml_hammer endpoint. User-uploaded YAML files are deserialized using yaml.load() without specifying a safe Loader on line 329. The yaml.load() function defaults to the full Loader in PyYAML versions before 5.1, which can instantiate arbitrary Python objects. An attacker can upload a YAML file containing !!python/object/apply directives to execute arbitrary code and achieve Remote Code Execution.

XML External Entity (XXE) injection vulnerability in /xxe_uploader endpoint. User-uploaded DOCX files are parsed with Document(file_path) on line 303 without XXE protections. DOCX files are ZIP archives containing XML. Maliciously crafted OOXML documents with external entity references can read local files or perform SSRF attacks. The endpoint name explicitly indicates intentional XXE vulnerability for training.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: Insecure JWT verification function. The insecure_verify() function on line 96-99 decodes JWT tokens with verify=False, completely disabling signature verification. This function is used in the /get/<cust_id> endpoint on line 227, allowing attackers to forge arbitrary JWT tokens and bypass authentication controls.

cwe: CWE-347: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature

extra.message: Broken Access Control / IDOR vulnerability in /get/<cust_id> endpoint. Customer records can be retrieved by any authenticated user by simply changing the cust_id parameter. No authorization checks verify that the authenticated user owns the customer record being requested. Combined with insecure_verify() which bypasses JWT signature validation, attackers can access any customer's sensitive data including CCN.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Broken Access Control / IDOR vulnerability in /fetch/customer endpoint. Customer records can be retrieved by specifying any customer_id in the JSON request body on line 207. No authorization checks verify that the authenticated user is allowed to access that specific customer record. An attacker can enumerate all customer records and access sensitive data including credit card numbers by iterating through customer IDs.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: Hardcoded cryptographic secrets in application code. Multiple hardcoded secret keys are defined: SECRET_KEY_HMAC='secret' on line 26, SECRET_KEY_HMAC_2='am0r3C0mpl3xK3y' on line 27, and secret_key on line 28. These are used for JWT signing and session management. Hardcoded secrets can be extracted from source code repositories and should be stored in environment variables or secure secret management systems.

Default hardcoded administrative credentials in setup code. The application creates a default admin user with username='admin' and password='admin123' on lines 62-63. These credentials are trivially guessable and should never be hardcoded. An attacker can immediately gain administrative access to the application.

Sensitive data exposure via API responses. Customer endpoints return credit card numbers (ccn field) in JSON responses on line 209-212. Even though responses are JSON, sensitive payment card data should not be transmitted in responses unless absolutely necessary and only to authorized clients over encrypted connections. Additionally, no PCI DSS compliance measures are evident.

cwe: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
